feed.title: Dave Liu — Blog
feed.subtitle: Short, honest notes on building software, working with people, and navigating a career.

entry.title: Why I'm Keeping a Public Work Journal
entry.summary: Starting a small public blog to think out loud about engineering, building, and career.
entry.content:
Placeholder / example post. This exists so the blog build pipeline has a real publish: true post to render end-to-end. Dave: edit or replace this with your real first post before merging the blog to the live site.

I've kept private notes for years. This is an experiment in publishing a thin, curated slice of them — the thoughts on building software, working with people, and navigating a career that I'd be comfortable saying out loud.

The rules I'm setting for myself:

  • Opt-in, not opt-out. A note only becomes a post when I explicitly mark it publish: true. Everything else stays private by default.
  • Synced from where I already write. Posts live in my notes vault, so publishing is a side effect of thinking, not a separate chore.
  • Short and honest over polished and frequent.

If you're reading this, the pipeline works.
